key_insight: GLOW-FDG is an open-source whole-body PET/CT model that automatically segments cancer lesions across multiple tumor types with strong generalization and fewer false positives.
clinical_relevance: It could speed up and standardize lesion quantification in routine oncology imaging, supporting more consistent tumor burden and total lesion glycolysis measurements.
method: Trained on 1,563 whole-body 18F-FDG PET/CT scans spanning multiple cancers
Validated on 185 external scans from independent institutions
Compared against public benchmark models and expert variability across breast, lung, head and neck cancer, and melanoma cohorts
evidence_signal: The abstract reports consistent outperformance on lesion detection, robust quantitative biomarker extraction, and evaluation across independent external cohorts, approaching inter-expert variability.
watch_item: Performance was shown on retrospective external datasets, so real-world deployment still needs prospective validation and workflow integration testing.
paper_title: GLOW-FDG: Generalized cancer LesiOn Whole-body segmentation model for $^{18}$F-FDG-PET/CT
